Oracle Events
- Search Airtable for Joaquin Reyes relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Name", value: "Joaquín Reyes" or search by Company "GreenStack Energy"). Find: recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle Phoenix to Seattle, In Progress, move dates April 11-19 2026, coordinator Javier Morales. Special requirements: 900-lb solar panel testing rig (8ft x 4ft x 3ft), requires custom padded crating, flatbed transport, freight elevator access at 4th floor lab. GreenStack HQ building full shutdown April 14-18 for electrical grid upgrades NO access, NO freight elevator, NO receiving dock. Delivery must happen by EOD Sunday April 13 OR Saturday April 19 at 10:00 AM or later (freight elevator available after facilities walkthrough).
- Search emails for GreenStack building shutdown details and vendor concerns using search_emails (query: "GreenStack" or "Joaquin" or "shutdown" or "building" or "rig" or similar). Find: (a) Derek Hollis's initial relocation request (greenstack_hazmat_reloc_request) with full shutdown schedule (April 14 at 6 AM through April 19 at 8 AM, freight elevator by 10 AM April 19), (b) Emeka Diallo's acknowledgment and strategy discussion (greenstack_hazmat_reloc_ack), (c) Derek's detailed specifications (greenstack_hazmat_reloc_details) confirming delivery windows and April 25 invoice deadline, (d) Chloe Vance's operational assessment (greenstack_chloe_ops_concerns) flagging Heartland's capacity limit (~500-600 lbs typical) and recommending Swift Relocations as alternative (has handled GreenStack equipment, familiar with Seattle HQ building/loading dock/freight elevator from Venkatesh move).
- Search Slack for any GreenStack or vendor coordination messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "GreenStack" or "Joaquin" or "rig" or "Swift" or similar). Find relevant operational discussions about vendor capacity, GreenStack relocations, and coordinator assignments.
- Search QuickBooks for the GreenStack invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria matching GreenStack Energy or INV-2026-0418) or quickbooks_search_bills. Find: INV-2026-0418, total $9,440, due April 25 2026 (GreenStack fiscal year-end deadline). Joaquin's line items: apartment sourcing $1,000, flight booking $20, moving coordination $500, overweight/specialty equipment surcharge $950 (900-lb rig, custom crating, flatbed, freight elevator coordination). Also find Mei-Lin Zhao's line items on the same invoice.
- Search CRM for GreenStack deal status using crm_search_companies (name: "GreenStack") and crm_list_engagements or crm_search_deals. Find: deal_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes ($9,440, In Progress, close date April 25). Verify account manager is Emeka Diallo, HR contact is Derek Hollis (derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com).
- Search Linear for GreenStack or Joaquin Reyes tracking tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Joaquin" or "GreenStack" or "Reyes" or "rig" or similar). Find: issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack (DOT Docs Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig, status: Done, not hazmat but overweight transport permit flagged). Check for any open apartment search, flight booking, or moving coordination tickets.
- Search Airtable for Tyler Okonkwo relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Name", value: "Tyler Okonkwo" or search by Company "Canopy Health"). Find: recTylerOkonkwoPhillyDenver Philadelphia to Denver, In Progress, move dates April 12-14 2026 (adjusted from April 11-13 to resolve coordinator overlap). Coordinator: Javier Morales. Denver apartment: 2150 Lawrence St Apt 4B, lease starts May 1. Must vacate Philadelphia by April 18. Storage gap: goods arrive Denver ~April 14 through lease start May 1 (~17 days of storage needed; Airtable notes "approx. 13-day gap" counting from vacate date April 18). High-value items: carbon-frame road bike (~$3,500), 65" TV. Household goods ~450 cu ft from 2BR apartment. $600 temporary storage surcharge PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL.
- Search emails for Tyler Okonkwo storage and Canopy Health approval status using search_emails (query: "Tyler Okonkwo" or "Canopy" or "storage" or "surcharge" or "Patricia" or similar). Find: Canopy Health Q2 wave coordination emails showing $2,650 in total pending surcharges across 3 relocations (Tyler's $600 storage + Marcus Webb's $1,200 cold-chain + Dr. Kovacs's $850 customs). Vendor commitments on hold pending Patricia Langford's written approval. Note: the April 7 approval deadline is primarily documented in CRM engagement notes (see CRM OE below) but may also appear in email threads about the Q2 wave.
- Search QuickBooks for Canopy Health invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria matching Canopy Health or INV-2026-0401). Find: INV-2026-0401, total $9,210 for Q2 wave (Tyler Okonkwo + Marcus Webb + Dr. Renata Kovacs), due May 4 2026. Tyler's line items include temp storage surcharge $600 (PENDING APPROVAL).
- Search CRM for Canopy Health account context using crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy") and crm_list_engagements. Find: engagement notes documenting Q2 wave, surcharge approval workflow, and April 7 deadline for Patricia Langford sign-off. Account manager: Catalina Dubois.
- Search Linear for Tyler Okonkwo or Canopy tracking tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Tyler" or "Canopy" or "Okonkwo" or similar). Find any relevant tracking issues for the Q2 wave or storage coordination (e.g., Canopy-related apartment search, moving coordination, or wave management tickets).
- Look up Chloe Vance's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe Vance") to find chloe.vance@moveops.com for the summary email.
- Send consolidated status email to Chloe Vance from Javier using send_email (sender: javier.morales@moveops.com, recipients: ["chloe.vance@moveops.com"]). Content should cover: (1) GreenStack/Joaquin Reyes building shutdown April 14-18, freight elevator available April 19 at 10 AM, two feasible delivery windows (by April 13 or April 19+), Heartland capacity concern for 900-lb rig, recommendation to use Swift instead, invoice deadline April 25; (2) Tyler Okonkwo/Canopy storage gap ~17 days (April 14 to May 1), $600 surcharge pending Patricia Langford approval, April 7 approval deadline already passed, vendor commitments still on hold; (3) specific decisions needed from Chloe (vendor switch for rig, storage approval escalation).
- Post summary in #operations channel (C006) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006"). Content should cover both relocations' status, key blockers, and decisions pending.
- Add comments to relevant Linear tracking tickets using linear_create_comment on any open GreenStack or Canopy issues, documenting the logistics status and vendor/approval blockers.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

2 The email to Chloe (visible in the content parameter) identifies the GreenStack Seattle building shutdown window as April 14-18, 2026, with the freight elevator becoming available on April 19 at 10:00 AM (or similar description of the shutdown schedule and elevator resumption).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #3The email content explicitly states: 'Shutdown starts: Monday, April 14 at 6:00 AM — all power cut, building secured' and 'Access resumes: Saturday, April 19 at 8:00 AM — freight elevator available by 10:00 AM'. The shutdown window April 14-18 and freight elevator resumption at 10:00 AM on April 19 are clearly identified.
✓Run #4The email content explicitly states: 'Shutdown begins: Monday, April 14 at 6:00 AM', 'Access resumes: Saturday, April 19 at 8:00 AM', and 'Freight elevator available: Saturday, April 19 at 10:00 AM'. This matches the required shutdown schedule and elevator resumption details.
✓Run #5The email content explicitly states 'Shutdown begins: Monday, April 14 at 6:00 AM' and 'Shutdown ends: Saturday, April 19 at 8:00 AM — facilities walkthrough first, freight elevator available by 10:00 AM', matching the required shutdown window.
✓Run #6The email content explicitly states: 'NO access April 14–18 — no exceptions, electrical contractor has exclusive site control' and 'Access resumes: Saturday, April 19 at 8:00 AM — facilities walkthrough first, with freight elevator access available by 10:00 AM'.
3 The email to Chloe (visible in the content parameter) identifies two feasible delivery windows for the 900-lb solar rig: by end of day April 13 (before shutdown) or April 19 at 10:00 AM or later (after shutdown).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email explicitly identifies two delivery windows: '(A) Before EOD Sunday April 13' and '(B) Saturday April 19 at 10:00 AM or later', directly matching the required feasible windows.
✓Run #2The email explicitly presents both options: 'Option A: Deliver by EOD Sunday, April 13 (before shutdown)' and 'Option B: Deliver Saturday, April 19 at 10:00 AM or later (after shutdown).'
✓Run #3The email clearly identifies 'Window A: By EOD Sunday, April 13 (before shutdown)' and 'Window B: Saturday, April 19 at 10:00 AM or later (after shutdown)' as the two feasible delivery windows.
✓Run #4The email clearly presents both options: 'Option 1: Deliver by EOD Sunday, April 13' (before shutdown) and 'Option 2: Deliver Saturday, April 19 at 10:00 AM or later' (after shutdown), with a recommendation for Option 2.
✓Run #5The email lists 'OPTION A: Complete delivery by EOD Sunday, April 13 (before shutdown)' and 'OPTION B: Saturday, April 19 at 10:00 AM or later (after shutdown, when freight elevator comes back online)'.
✓Run #6The email content explicitly lists 'Window 1: By EOD Sunday, April 13 (already passed)' and 'Window 2: Saturday, April 19 at 10:00 AM or later (this is the viable option as of today)' as the two feasible delivery windows.
4 The email to Chloe (visible in the content parameter) recommends Swift Relocations for the rig and provides a specific justification discovered from investigation such as Swift's familiarity with the GreenStack Seattle HQ building from the Priya Venkatesh move, or Swift's heavy equipment capability including flatbed with liftgate (or similar discovered justification).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email recommends Swift Relocations with specific justification: 'their Seattle team is already familiar with the GreenStack HQ building (loading dock, freight elevator) from the Priya Venkatesh move' and notes Heartland's 500-600 lb capacity limit.
✓Run #2The email recommends switching to Swift Relocations and provides specific justification: 'Swift Relocations has heavy equipment experience and their Seattle team is already familiar with the GreenStack HQ building (loading dock, freight elevator, etc.) from the Priya Venkatesh move earlier this month' and 'Swift also has the flatbed + liftgate capability needed for this weight class.'
✓Run #3The email explicitly recommends Swift Relocations and states: 'Swift has the heavy equipment capability (flatbed with liftgate) and their Seattle team is already familiar with the GreenStack HQ building from the Priya Venkatesh move.' Both discovered justifications (equipment capability and building familiarity from the Priya Venkatesh move) are included.
✓Run #4The email recommends switching to Swift Relocations and provides specific justification: Heartland's fleet tops out at 500-600 lbs (insufficient for 900-lb rig), Swift has heavy equipment experience, and 'their Seattle team is already familiar with the GreenStack HQ building (loading dock, freight elevator) from the Priya Venkatesh move.'
✓Run #5The email recommends Swift Relocations and specifically justifies it: 'Swift's Seattle team is already familiar with the GreenStack HQ building (loading dock, freight elevator) from the Priya Venkatesh move. They have the heavy equipment capability.'
✓Run #6The email recommends Swift Relocations with justification: 'Swift Relocations is the recommended alternative — they have heavy equipment experience and their Seattle team is already familiar with the GreenStack HQ building (loading dock, freight elevator, etc.) from the Priya Venkatesh move earlier this year.' This matches discovered email evidence from Chloe to Emeka.
5 The email to Chloe (visible in the content parameter) identifies the GreenStack invoice deadline as April 25, 2026.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email states 'Invoice INV-2026-0418 ($9,440) is due April 25 per GreenStack's fiscal year-end', clearly identifying the April 25 deadline.
